codeReview.summary: The feature produces a comprehensive, well-structured configuration and feature-toggle index at docs/platform/config-feature-toggles.md (457 lines) linked from docs/INDEX.md. It covers all 11 required areas (security, licensing, mail/notification, export-approval, module gating, runtime gating, integration, AI-related/usage-metrics/admission, inventory-accounting-event, auto-approval, and orchestrator switches) with scope classifications, defaults, consumers, and operational caveats. The commit is docs-only (no code changes). All 8 cross-referenced documents exist. Three minor accuracy issues were identified but none are blocking.

issues.description: SMTP validation description is inaccurate: docs say SmtpPropertiesValidator 'logs a warning' but the actual code throws IllegalStateException (crashing the app) when SMTP config is incomplete in production. The validator only logs/skips when erp.mail.enabled=false. In production (prod & !seed profile), missing SMTP config is a startup failure, not a warning.

mail.enabled default discrepancy: The docs state 'default true' for erp.mail.enabled, but EmailProperties.java has 'private boolean enabled = false' (default false). The @Value annotations in SmtpPropertiesValidator and RequiredConfigHealthIndicator use ':true' as the Spring property default, which overrides the ConfigurationProperties class default. The effective runtime default is true (Spring overrides), so the doc is operationally correct, but the nuance between ConfigurationProperties class default and @Value annotation default could be confusing to maintainers reading the source.

SALES module path prefixes in the docs show '/api/v1/credit/*' but the actual ModuleGatingInterceptor maps SALES to '/api/v1/credit/override-requests' and '/api/v1/credit/limit-requests' specifically, not a wildcard. The docs use a glob-style notation that is slightly misleading — it implies all /api/v1/credit/* paths map to SALES, but the interceptor only checks those two specific sub-paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: TenantRuntimeAccessService uses different property names (erp.tenant.runtime.default.max-concurrent-requests, erp.tenant.runtime.default.max-requests-per-minute) and different defaults (0, 0) compared to TenantRuntimeEnforcementService (erp.tenant.runtime.default-max-concurrent-requests, erp.tenant.runtime.default-max-requests-per-minute with defaults 200, 5000). The config-feature-toggles docs only documents the TenantRuntimeEnforcementService properties. This dual-property-name situation is a potential source of confusion for maintainers and could be worth recording in the library.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Compare TenantRuntimeAccessService.java lines 79-83 (@Value erp.tenant.runtime.default.max-concurrent-requests:0, erp.tenant.runtime.default.max-requests-per-minute:0) vs TenantRuntimeEnforcementService.java lines 62-66 (@Value erp.tenant.runtime.default-max-concurrent-requests:200, erp.tenant.runtime.default-max-requests-per-minute:5000). The docs section 2.1 only lists the EnforcementService properties.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The documentation correctly follows the 'Last reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD' freshness convention and the VAL-PLAT-007 assertion reference pattern established by prior docs packets in this mission. The worker adhered to the mission AGENTS.md rule of keeping packet scope narrow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Line 3 of config-feature-toggles.md has 'Last reviewed: 2026-03-30', and line 7 has 'It fulfills VAL-PLAT-007.'

summary: PASS — The config-feature-toggles documentation is comprehensive, well-organized, and covers all required surfaces. The 457-line packet is linked from docs/INDEX.md and fulfills VAL-PLAT-007. All 8 cross-references resolve to existing documents. Three non-blocking accuracy issues were identified: (1) SmtpPropertiesValidator startup behavior described as 'logs a warning' when it actually throws IllegalStateException in prod, (2) a subtle nuance between EmailProperties class default (false) and @Value annotation default (true) for erp.mail.enabled, and (3) SALES module path prefix notation using wildcard '/api/v1/credit/*' instead of the actual specific sub-paths. None of these are blocking — the documentation is operationally useful and accurate enough for its stated purpose of helping maintainers find platform knobs.
